title: Definition of a Function вАФ Medium

questions.question: A function g is defined by g(x)= x^{2}вИТ5x. Find the value of g(k+1) in terms of k.
questions.explanation:
g(k+1)= (k+1)^{2}вИТ5(k+1)=($k^{2}$+2k+1)вИТ5kвИТ5= k^{2}вИТ3kвИТ4.

**Common mistake:** Failing to expand $(k+1)^{2}$correctly as $k^{2}$+2k+1 or incorrectly distributing the вИТ5 across the brackets.

questions.question: Find the range of the function f(x)=12вИТ4x for the domain вИТ2вЙ§xвЙ§3.
questions.explanation:
Evaluate the endpoints: f(вИТ2)=12вИТ4(вИТ2)=20 and f(3)=12вИТ4(3)=0. The range is .

**Common mistake:** Students may provide the range in the wrong order or only evaluate one endpoint.

questions.question: The function h is defined by h(x)= \frac{2}{x+2} for x \neq вИТ2. Find the value of a such that h(a)=4.
questions.explanation:
Solve 20/(a+2)=4. Multiplying gives 20=4(a+2) \implies 5=a+2 \implies a=3.

**Common mistake:** Common errors include multiplying the numerator by the denominator incorrectly or substituting 4 into the function instead of equating it.

questions.question:
Which of these domains would ensure that the mapping f:x$\mapsto

$$x^{2}$+1 is a one-to-one function?$
questions.explanation:
A quadratic is many-to-one over all real numbers. Restricting it to values on one side of the vertex (x=0) makes it one-to-one.

**Common mistake:** Assuming all algebraic functions are one-to-one regardless of the symmetry of the curve.

questions.question: State the range of the function f(x)= x^{2}+4 for the domain xвЙ•вИТ1.
questions.explanation:
The vertex of $x^{2}$+4 is at (0,4). Since x=0 is within the domain xвЙ•вИТ1, the minimum value is 4. For large x, y increases infinitely.

**Common mistake:** Students often check only the boundary x=вИТ1 and conclude the range is f(x)вЙ•5, missing the lower vertex point.
